title: Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan says: In past company made lot of mistakes, including not changing the reporting structure of the team that…
summary: Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan admitted that the company made significant mistakes in the past, primarily due to a flawed reporting structure where engineers were disconnected from the CEO. Tan has since overhauled this by having all engineers report directly to him, simplifying the product roadmap, and bringing back former Intel veterans to address the decline in the data center business. He also noted improvements in Intel 18A yields and confirmed that 14A volume production will align with TSMC's A14 in 2029.

title: AI-heavy USNZ ETF volume surges 24-fold on Nvidia
summary: The Xtrackers Net Zero Pathway Paris Aligned US Equity ETF (USNZ), which is heavily invested in AI and mega-cap tech companies, experienced a 24-fold surge in trading volume. This significant activity is attributed to various AI-related developments and analyst calls concerning its top holdings, including Nvidia, Apple, Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, and Broadcom. The article highlights market sentiment around these companies, such as Nvidia's strong demand for Blackwell chips and upcoming earnings, and Apple's mixed reviews regarding its AI upside being priced in.

title: Extra Space Storage adds two directors to board By Investing.com
summary: Extra Space Storage Inc. (NYSE:EXR) has appointed Crystal Call Maggelet and RJ Pittman to its board of directors, increasing the board to ten members and ensuring nine are independent. Maggelet, CEO of FJ Management Inc., joins the Nominating, Governance, & Corporate Responsibility Committee, while Pittman, former CEO of Matterport, will serve on the Audit Committee. The company, a REIT with a market capitalization of $31.4 billion, recently outperformed Q1 2026 earnings expectations and declared a dividend of $1.62 per share.

title: Is Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) a Value Play Amidst Market Volatility
summary: Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) is trading at $703,154.50, maintaining a $1.01 trillion market capitalization despite recent volatility. Valuation models show mixed results, with some suggesting undervaluation by up to 37.8% and others deeming it fairly valued. The company's significant $373 billion cash pile offers strategic optionality for future acquisitions under new CEO Greg Abel, making it a compelling "Hold" or "Buy on dips" for long-term investors.

title: Lido Advisors Boosts Stake in Invesco BulletShares 2027 Corporate Bond ETF
summary: Lido Advisors significantly increased its holding in the Invesco BulletShares 2027 Corporate Bond ETF (BSCR) by 3,854,896 shares, making it the firm's second-largest holding, valued at $1.4 billion. This move reflects a strategic defensive positioning by the investment advisor, aiming for predictable cash flows in an uncertain interest rate environment. The target-maturity bond fund tracks the Invesco BulletShares Corporate Bond 2027 Index and offers a low expense ratio, maturing in December 2027.

title: Luke Wood Joins Snap Inc. Board of Directors
summary: Snap Inc. announced the appointment of Luke Wood, former President of Beats by Dr. Dre and Vice President at Apple Inc., to its board of directors, effective May 20, 2026. CEO Evan Spiegel and Chairperson Michael Lynton both expressed enthusiasm for Wood's experience in technology and culture. Wood, who is also CEO of Violet St Holdings, LLC and serves on the board of Fender Musical Instruments Corp., expressed excitement to contribute to Snap's innovation.

title: Apple shares hit new all-time closing high
summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) shares reached a new all-time closing high of $32.25 on Wednesday, May 20, 2026, with an intraday high of $302.80. The company's market value stands at $4.439 trillion, making it the world's third-most valuable company after NVIDIA and Alphabet. The article also provides a list of market values for other selected companies.

title: PayPal Holdings Inc. (PYPL): Michael Burry Initiates New Position
summary: Michael Burry, the legendary investor, has initiated a new position in PayPal Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:PYPL), making it 3.5% of his portfolio. Burry views PYPL as a favorite in the software payments sector and believes that the recent sell-off in software stocks due to changing market demands will not last. Despite challenges from AI and alternative payment methods, PayPal continues to maintain a growing customer base.

title: Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) Stock Split on Wall Street as Record Q2 Results Fuel Upgrade While Valuation Prompts Caution
summary: Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) is experiencing diverging analyst opinions despite record Q2 results, driven by strong revenue and the announcement of a new CEO, John Ternus. While some analysts are bullish due to the positive performance and leadership transition, others express caution regarding the stock's high valuation and potential risks in key markets and supply chains. The consensus highlights the company's strong performance but suggests that the current valuation may already reflect most of the good news, limiting short-term upside.

title: Hertz Stock Flashes Golden Cross After Philippe Laffont Opens New Position - Hertz Global Holdings (NASDA
summary: Hertz Global Holdings (NASDAQ: HTZ) stock has shown early signs of stabilization, including a "Golden Cross" chart pattern, after Coatue Management's Philippe Laffont opened a new position in the company. While the Golden Cross is a bullish technical indicator, Hertz still faces previous challenges like electric-vehicle depreciation and volatile used-car pricing. Laffont's investment, from a firm known for tech-focused investing, adds an interesting dynamic to Hertz's recovery prospects.

title: Apple Inc. (AAPL): A Top Stock Pick by Graham Stephan?
summary: Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) is featured on Insider Monkey's list of Graham Stephan's top stock picks, primarily due to its prevalence in index funds Stephan recommends. The company reported a record-breaking Q2 2026 revenue of $111.2 billion, a 17% year-over-year increase, with diluted EPS rising 22% to $2.01. Despite Apple's strong performance, Insider Monkey suggests other AI stocks may offer higher returns in a shorter timeframe.

title: 8 Cool New iOS 27 Accessibility Features You Can Look Forward To In The Fall
summary: Apple is set to introduce a range of new accessibility features with iOS 27, expected to be previewed at WWDC 2026 and released in the fall. These updates leverage Apple Intelligence to enhance existing features like VoiceOver, Magnifier, and Voice Controls, making iPhones, iPads, Macs, Apple TVs, and Apple Vision Pros more accessible. Key improvements include advanced image descriptions, natural language understanding for device control, intelligent document reading, auto-generated subtitles for personal videos, global name recognition, a new FaceTime API for sign language interpreters, and expanded game controller support.

title: 8 Cool New iOS 27 Accessibility Features You Can Look Forward To In The Fall
summary: Apple is set to introduce a range of new accessibility features with iOS 27, expected to be unveiled at WWDC 2026 and released in the fall. These enhancements leverage Apple Intelligence to improve features like VoiceOver and Magnifier for blind and low-vision users, and bring natural language processing to Voice Controls for easier device interaction. Additionally, the update will include an improved Accessibility Reader for complex documents, auto-generated subtitles for personal videos, enhanced Name Recognition, a new FaceTime API for sign language interpreters, and expanded game controller support.

title: Trending Stocks Today | HCW Biologics Shoots up 151.88%
summary: The article highlights trending stocks on May 20th during regular trading hours, with HCW Biologics (HCWB.US) leading the gainers by soaring 151.88%. Other significant movers included Micware (MWC.US) and Silexion Therapeutics (SLXN.US). The "Trending Stocks Today" feature identifies stocks using capitalization, turnover ratio, and percentage change, adjusting criteria for small-cap versus large-cap stocks.

title: Hesai Becomes Strategic LiDAR Partner of Mercedes-Benz, Will Supply Products for L3 Autonomous Driving Models
summary: Hesai Technology has partnered with Mercedes-Benz to supply LiDAR products for its L3 autonomous driving models in both Europe and China, with production handled by Hesai's new "Galileo" manufacturing center in Thailand. The company reported its fourth consecutive quarter of profitability in Q1 2026, with revenue up 29.6% year-on-year and LiDAR deliveries surging by 140.9%. Hesai also announced a strategic restructuring into "LiDAR Business" and "Strategic Growth Initiatives," and holds a leading market share in main ADAS LiDAR shipments globally.

title: Should You Invest in Gold or the S&P 500? It Depends.
summary: This article compares investing in gold through SPDR Gold Shares (GLD) versus investing in the S&P 500 through State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY). While SPY is generally more affordable and income-generating, GLD has shown stronger recent performance and lower volatility, serving as a hedge against market instability. The authors suggest that a diversified portfolio might best leverage both strategies.

title: Should You Invest in Gold or the S&P 500? It Depends.
summary: This article analyzes whether to invest in gold (SPDR Gold Shares, GLD) or the S&P 500 (State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust, SPY), highlighting their differences in cost, risk, and portfolio role. While SPY offers a lower expense ratio and dividends, GLD has shown stronger recent performance and lower volatility. The article suggests that a diversified approach using both may be the smartest investment strategy.

title: Apple uses AI and human reviews to prevent $2.2B in potential App Store fraud
summary: Apple successfully prevented over $2.2 billion in potential App Store fraud in 2025 by employing a combination of artificial intelligence tools and human reviewers. This initiative also stopped the use of more than 5.4 million stolen credit cards. The efforts highlight Apple's commitment to securing its digital marketplace.

title: Trump Has Been Investing in Companies and Then Pumping Them in His Speeches
summary: Former President Trump has been found to invest in companies and then promote them in his public speeches, often on the same day as his stock purchases. This pattern of behavior, which includes investments in Thermo Fisher Scientific, Apple, Micron, and Dell, raises significant ethical concerns about financial self-dealing and a potential conflict of interest. Despite claims that his trades are managed by independent advisors, multiple "unsolicited" transactions suggest his direct involvement, leading to bipartisan calls for legislation to prevent such activity by elected officials.

title: Pfizer Stock Shares $49 Bil Success With Investors
summary: Pfizer (PFE) has returned $49 billion to shareholders over the last five years through dividends and buybacks, making it the 30th highest in history for shareholder returns. This strategy, though attractive for direct returns, prompts questions about sacrificing growth, especially given Pfizer's recent revenue shocks and slower growth compared to companies like Meta and Microsoft. The article emphasizes the importance of staying invested and suggests a diversified portfolio approach to manage risk and secure returns.

title: Walleye Partners LLC Buys New Position in Apple Inc. $AAPL
summary: Walleye Partners LLC has acquired a new position in Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) during the fourth quarter, purchasing 2,507 shares valued at approximately $682,000. This investment makes Apple the 20th largest holding for Walleye Partners, accounting for 0.8% of its total portfolio. The article highlights strong institutional interest in Apple, positive analyst ratings with a "Moderate Buy" consensus, and recent strong financial performance, including a dividend increase.

title: MP Materials (MP) Valuation Check After Apple Deal And Volatile Reaction To Record First Quarter
summary: MP Materials (MP) shares are experiencing volatility despite an Apple partnership and strong Q1 earnings. While a popular narrative suggests the stock is 8% overvalued at $54.93, anticipating future high margins from industrial manufacturing, Simply Wall St's DCF model indicates it might be undervalued by 62.1% at that price. The article highlights the contrasting valuation perspectives and the importance of execution for MP Materials' transformation.

title: KLP Kapitalforvaltning AS Has $1.58 Billion Stock Holdings in Apple Inc. $AAPL
summary: KLP Kapitalforvaltning AS holds a significant stake in Apple Inc. ($AAPL), valued at $1.58 billion, making it their second-largest holding despite a minor 0.3% reduction in Q4. Institutional interest in Apple remains strong, with a majority of the stock owned by institutional investors and several recent additions to their positions. The company shows positive fundamentals with strong earnings, a dividend increase, and a bullish analyst consensus, though it faces some regulatory and competitive challenges.

title: Momentum Wealth Planning LLC Has $4.47 Million Stake in Apple Inc. $AAPL
summary: Momentum Wealth Planning LLC significantly increased its stake in Apple Inc. (AAPL) by 504.7% in the fourth quarter, now holding 16,443 shares valued at approximately $4.47 million, making it 3.5% of their portfolio. Apple recently reported strong quarterly results, exceeding EPS and revenue estimates, and raised its quarterly dividend. Despite some insider selling and mixed analyst opinions, the stock maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus and trades near record highs.

title: Apple Inc. $AAPL Shares Sold by Boston Trust Walden Corp
summary: Boston Trust Walden Corp reduced its stake in Apple by 2.9% in the fourth quarter, selling 45,840 shares, though Apple remains its second-largest holding. Despite this institutional selling and recent insider sales by CEO Tim Cook and CFO Kevan Parekh, Apple reported strong quarterly earnings, raised its dividend, and maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average price target of $308.74. The company is also seeing positive sentiment around its services business and new AI features.

title: Columbia Asset Management Sells 3,704 Shares of Apple Inc. $AAPL
summary: Columbia Asset Management reduced its stake in Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) by 1.8% during the fourth quarter, selling 3,704 shares and ending with 199,639 shares valued at $54.27 million. Despite this reduction, Apple remains Columbia Asset Management's largest holding, accounting for approximately 9.6% of its portfolio. Several other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in Apple, while insiders sold a total of 97,759 shares over the last 90 days.

title: Apple Inc. $AAPL Shares Acquired by Alphinity Investment Management Pty Ltd
summary: Alphinity Investment Management Pty Ltd significantly increased its stake in Apple Inc. ($AAPL) by 105.7% in the fourth quarter, now holding 1.5 million shares valued at approximately $406.5 million. This move is indicative of broader institutional interest, with hedge funds and asset managers collectively owning 67.73% of Apple's shares. The article also highlights Apple's strong performance, including being near its 52-week high, beating earnings estimates, and a recent dividend increase, despite some regulatory and competitive pressures.

title: UGREEN Unveils Nexode & MagFlow Air Editions: Pocket Power That Packs a Punch
summary: UGREEN has launched its new Nexode and MagFlow Air Editions, a series of ultra-portable charging solutions specifically designed for Apple users. These editions emphasize compact design, powerful performance, and reliable safety for fast charging on the go. The lineup includes Nexode Air 65W and 45W chargers for MacBooks and other Apple devices, as well as MagFlow Air Magnetic Power Banks in 10000mAh and 5000mAh capacities for magnetic wireless charging.

title: iShares MSCI USA Quality GARP ETF
summary: This article provides detailed information about the iShares MSCI USA Quality GARP ETF (GARP), including its price, performance metrics, holdings, expense ratio, and ESG scores. The ETF tracks the MSCI USA Quality GARP Select Index and focuses on growth-oriented U.S. equities. It also offers comparative data against peer ETFs in various categories like volatility, dividends, and expense ratios.

title: What Does HP Inc.'s Latest Dividend Declaration Signify for Investors?
summary: HP Inc. (HPQ) has declared a consistent quarterly dividend of $0.30 per share for fiscal year 2026, signaling confidence in its financial stability despite a challenging market. The company offers an attractive forward dividend yield of 5.7% and boasts a 16-year streak of dividend increases, supported by sustainable payout ratios and strong free cash flow. While HP faces market headwinds and some earnings volatility, its strategic shift towards AI-powered devices and cost-cutting initiatives aim to stabilize future performance and shareholder returns, making it an interesting prospect for income-focused investors looking for high yield and low valuation.

title: CLSA Upgrades Marvell Technology, Inc. (MRVL.US) and Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD.US) to Outperform, Raises Multiple TPs; NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA.US) Seen Regaining Anthropic Share
summary: CLSA has upgraded Marvell Technology, Inc. (MRVL.US) and Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD.US) from Hold to Outperform, while maintaining Outperform ratings on Broadcom Inc. (AVGO.US) and Apple Inc. (AAPL.US) and reiterating Conviction List Buy ratings on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (2330.TW) and NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA.US). The broker also raised price targets for several major US tech and semiconductor stocks, seeing potential for NVIDIA to regain market share at Anthropic despite current computing resource shortages and the significant deployment of competitor chips. CLSA anticipates severe computing shortages into 2026-2027 and does not view current semiconductor valuations as expensive, believing the rally is just beginning.

title: Lumentum EVP Vincent Retort sells $3.04 million in stock
summary: Lumentum Holdings Inc.'s EVP, Vincent Retort, sold 3,183 shares of common stock for $3.04 million on May 18, 2026, under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. Additionally, he disposed of 3,299 shares to cover tax obligations relating to restricted stock units. Despite an InvestingPro analysis suggesting overvaluation, Lumentum recently surpassed Q3 earnings expectations, was added to the Nasdaq-100 Index, and received a raised price target from BofA Securities due to an improved margin outlook.

title: Alphabet Inc. Class A Trade Ideas — AQUISUK:ABEAD
summary: This article compiles various trade ideas and analyses for Alphabet Inc. Class A (GOOGL), focusing on its technical and fundamental outlook. Multiple contributors analyze GOOGL's chart patterns, support/resistance levels, MACD, RSI, and Gamma Exposure (GEX) to project potential price movements and suggest trading strategies such as bull-call spreads and layered entries. The prevailing sentiment across most analyses is bullish, anticipating further upside movement for the stock, particularly if key resistance levels are breached.

title: Tile vs. AirTag™: Which Bluetooth Tracker Should You Choose?
summary: This article compares Tile Bluetooth trackers with Apple AirTag, highlighting key differences to help consumers choose the right device. It delves into aspects like compatibility, price, Bluetooth range, design, battery life, water resistance, and accessory requirements for both products. The comparison aims to inform users about features such as Tile's multi-platform support and diverse designs versus AirTag's Apple-exclusive ecosystem and Precision Finding.

title: Apple Reportedly Plans Hardware Team Shake Up To Ramp Up Future Devices As It Gears Up For Incoming CEO John Ternus
summary: Apple's Chief Hardware Officer, Johny Srouji, is reportedly reorganizing the company's hardware team to enhance product development and better integrate teams working on in-house silicon. This shake-up comes ahead of the Worldwide Developers Conference, where Apple is expected to announce major software updates and where current CEO Tim Cook will deliver his final keynote before John Ternus takes over. The reorganization includes new leadership for product design and reflects a strategic move to prepare for future device innovations under the incoming CEO.

title: Low Volatility ETF (FDLO) Touches a New 52-Week High
summary: The Fidelity Low Volatility Factor ETF (FDLO) has reached a new 52-week high, driven by investor shifts towards defensive assets amid inflation concerns and the fund's significant holdings in AI-benefiting technology giants. The ETF, which focuses on large and mid-capitalization U.S. companies with lower volatility, charges 15 basis points in annual fees and shows potential for further gains with a positive weighted alpha of 15.17.

title: The Meta warning: When AI spending becomes a liability
summary: Meta's recent Q1 earnings report, revealing a significant increase in capital expenditures for AI, caused its stock to drop by 10%. This event highlights a growing concern that unchecked AI spending can become a liability if not tied to measurable value and sustainable investment frameworks. CIOs are urged to implement disciplined oversight, set realistic ROI timelines, and build strong business cases for AI investments to avoid accumulating technical debt and ensure their strategies deliver tangible outcomes.

title: Apple Trades Close to 52-Week High: Buy, Sell or Hold the Stock?
summary: Apple (AAPL) stock is trading close to its 52-week high, driven by strong iPhone sales and growth in Services revenues, outperforming the broader Zacks Computer and Technology sector. Despite this performance, the company faces significant competition in the PC and smartphone markets and has experienced delays in its AI initiatives, impacting investor confidence. Analysts suggest Apple shares are currently overvalued, but future prospects could benefit from an expanding portfolio and a collaboration with Google on AI features.

title: Apple Trades Close to 52-Week High: Buy, Sell or Hold the Stock?
summary: Apple stock is trading near its 52-week high, benefiting from strong iPhone and Services revenue growth. However, the company faces intense competition in the PC, smartphone, and AI sectors, and its shares are considered overvalued. While an expanding product portfolio and a collaboration with Google for AI are positive outlooks, market saturation and high valuation suggest investors should hold rather than buy or sell at this time.

title: Apple Unveils AI Push Beyond Siri With Smart Writing, Accessibility Features
summary: Apple is expanding its AI capabilities beyond Siri, introducing new features focused on smart writing, accessibility, and communication tools. These updates, expected later this year, include advanced VoiceOver for image descriptions, upgraded Magnifier and Voice Control, and automatic subtitle generation. Apple also announced AI-driven wheelchair controls for Vision Pro users and confirmed its Worldwide Developers Conference for June 8-12, 2026, where major software and Apple Intelligence updates are anticipated.

title: Sony Launches $650 Headphones Made With Steel and Vegan Leather
summary: Sony Group Corp. has launched a new pair of high-end wireless headphones called "1000X The Collexion" for $650. This premium model features luxurious materials like stainless steel and a specially developed synthetic leather, setting a new price point for Sony in the headphone market. The company claims these headphones offer improved audio quality compared to their previous models.

title: Qualcomm shares slide as chip stocks retreat and investors refocus on Apple exposure risk
summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) shares declined by 5.6% due to a broader pullback in semiconductor stocks driven by macro concerns and persistent inflation. Investors are also wary of Qualcomm's long-term revenue exposure to Apple's iPhone modems, as Apple works on in-house modem solutions. Recent analyst rating changes and profit-taking in the AI/semiconductor sector may have further amplified the stock's drop despite no new Qualcomm-specific fundamental news.

title: NorthCoast Asset Management Boosts iShares iBonds Dec 2027 ETF Position
summary: NorthCoast Asset Management LLC increased its holding in the iShares iBonds Dec 2027 Term Treasury ETF (IBTH) during the first quarter of 2026, acquiring an additional 696,718 shares valued at $15.7 million. This strategic move expanded their IBTH stake to 1.4% of their total 13F assets under management, diversifying a portfolio previously concentrated in equities. The IBTH ETF, which holds government-backed U.S. Treasury securities maturing in December 2027, offers a predictable interest rate duration and a low expense ratio, making it suitable for corporate cash flow planning or fixed-income laddering strategies.

title: Why Is YieldMax AAPL Option Income Strategy ETF (APLY) Stock Down Today?
summary: The YieldMax AAPL Option Income Strategy ETF (APLY) is currently trading up by +0.36% today, despite the article title suggesting it is down. The stock is above its 50-day moving average but below its 200-day moving average, indicating short-term strength but long-term weakness. Meyka's AI assigns APLY a "C" grade and forecasts negative returns over various periods, including a -33.36% decline in one month and -16.87% in one year.

title: Qualcomm Inc Stock (QCOM) Moved Down by 3.72% on May 19: Key Drivers Unveiled
summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) stock dropped by 3.72% on May 19th due to renewed analyst skepticism, competitive pressures in the smartphone AI market, and profit-taking after a significant rally. Concerns about valuation, a competitor's rapid advancement in Agentic AI features, and recent insider selling contributed to the downward movement. Despite a "buy signal" from MACD, investors are cautious given the company's underperformance in the Technology Equipment sector.

title: Apple strikes talent and IP deal with virtual avatar startup Animato
summary: Apple has entered into a talent and intellectual property (IP) deal with Animato, a startup known for its virtual avatar software. This agreement allows Apple to hire Animato employees and gain a non-exclusive license to Animato's IP and patent applications. This strategic move enables Apple to access Animato's technology without a full acquisition, a common approach for tech companies, especially concerning AI startups, amidst increased regulatory scrutiny.

title: New Buy Rating for Palo Alto Networks (PANW), the Technology Giant
summary: Rosenblatt Securities analyst Catharine Trebnick maintained a Buy rating on Palo Alto Networks (PANW) with a price target of $275.00, aligning with a "Strong Buy" analyst consensus. The company reported strong Q1 earnings with $2.59 billion in revenue and $432 million in net profit, though corporate insider sentiment shows a negative trend with increased share selling.

title: Apple will subtitle videos and let Vision Pro help control power wheelchairs
summary: Apple is set to release a suite of new accessibility features later this year, many of which are powered by Apple Intelligence. These updates will enhance tools like VoiceOver, Magnifier, and Voice Control with more detailed descriptions and natural language navigation. Additionally, Apple Vision Pro users will gain the ability to control compatible power wheelchairs with their eyes, and the entire Apple ecosystem will support on-device generated subtitles for uncaptioned videos.

title: Epic Games' 'Fortnite' returns to App Stores worldwide
summary: Epic Games announced that its popular game "Fortnite" has returned to global App Stores, signaling confidence in a favorable outcome from its ongoing legal dispute with Apple. The company believes that once Apple is compelled to reveal its costs, governments worldwide will challenge Apple's "junk fees." Fortnite had previously returned to the U.S. App Store last year after a five-year ban, though it is not yet available in the Australian App Store.

title: Apple acquires company that develops avatar software specifically for use in video chats and lessons
summary: Apple has acquired Animato, a company focused on developing avatar software for video chats and lessons. The acquisition was disclosed by the European Union under its Digital Markets Act and grants Apple rights to Animato's employees, a non-exclusive license to its intellectual property, and its patent applications. Animato was founded in October 2022 by Francesco Rossi, a former Apple employee.
